pt_help_details.title: 1-Minute Push-ups
pt_help_details.section_content: You may rest in the up position, either on your hands and toes or by flexing your knees and resting on your lower legs. You may not lift your hands or feet from the floor.

pt_help_details.title: 1-Minute Push-ups
pt_help_details.section_content: If exempt, this component is excluded. The composite score is then calculated based only on the points earned in the remaining components, scaled to a 100-point maximum.

pt_help_details.title: 2-Minute Hand-Release Push-ups
pt_help_details.section_content:
Start in the prone position with hands flat on the ground. Push up until elbows are fully extended, then lower your body completely to the ground.

Lift both hands from the floor, then place them back down before starting the next repetition.

pt_help_details.title: 2-Minute Hand-Release Push-ups
pt_help_details.section_content: You may rest in the up or down position. In the down position, you may rest with your face on the ground, but you cannot lift your hands or feet.

pt_help_details.title: 2-Minute Hand-Release Push-ups
pt_help_details.section_content: The score is the total number of correct hand-release push-ups completed in two minutes.

pt_help_details.title: 1-Minute Sit-ups
pt_help_details.section_content: You may only rest in the up position.

pt_help_details.title: Forearm Plank
pt_help_details.section_content: The score is based on the total time you can maintain the correct plank position.

pt_help_details.title: Forearm Plank
pt_help_details.section_content: If exempt, this component is excluded. The composite score is then calculated based only on the points earned in the remaining components, scaled to a 100-point maximum.

pt_help_details.title: 1.5-Mile Run
pt_help_details.section_content: Complete a 1.5-mile run on a flat, measured course as quickly as possible.

pt_help_details.title: 1.5-Mile Run
pt_help_details.section_content: The score is based on the time taken to complete the run. A faster time results in a higher score.

pt_help_details.title: 1.5-Mile Run
pt_help_details.section_content: If exempt, this component is excluded. The composite score is then calculated based only on the points earned in the remaining components, scaled to a 100-point maximum.

pt_help_details.title: 2-km Walk
pt_help_details.section_content: Complete a 2-kilometer walk on a flat, measured course as quickly as possible. This is a pass/fail component.

pt_help_details.title: 2-Minute Hand-Release Push-ups
pt_help_details.section_content: If exempt, this component is excluded. The composite score is then calculated based only on the points earned in the remaining components, scaled to a 100-point maximum.

pt_help_details.title: 1-Minute Sit-ups
pt_help_details.section_content:
Start by lying on your back with your knees bent at a 90-degree angle. Your arms should be crossed over your chest with your hands on your shoulders. A spotter will hold your feet.

Raise your upper body until your elbows touch your knees or thighs, then lower your body until your shoulder blades touch the floor.

pt_help_details.title: 1-Minute Sit-ups
pt_help_details.section_content: If exempt, this component is excluded. The composite score is then calculated based only on the points earned in the remaining components, scaled to a 100-point maximum.

pt_help_details.title: Forearm Plank
pt_help_details.section_content: No resting is permitted during this component.

pt_help_details.title: 2-Minute Cross-Leg Reverse Crunches
pt_help_details.section_content:
Lie on your back with knees bent and feet on the floor. Cross one leg over the other and place your hands under your head.

Lift your hips and bring your knees toward your chest without lifting your upper back from the floor, then lower your hips back to the starting position.

pt_help_details.title: 2-Minute Cross-Leg Reverse Crunches
pt_help_details.section_content: You may rest at any point during the exercise.

pt_help_details.title: 2-Minute Cross-Leg Reverse Crunches
pt_help_details.section_content: The score is the total number of correct crunches completed in two minutes.

pt_help_details.title: 2-Minute Cross-Leg Reverse Crunches
pt_help_details.section_content: If exempt, this component is excluded. The composite score is then calculated based only on the points earned in the remaining components, scaled to a 100-point maximum.

pt_help_details.title: Forearm Plank
pt_help_details.section_content: Start with your elbows and forearms on the floor, parallel to your body. Your body should form a straight line from your head to your heels. Hold this position for as long as possible.

pt_help_details.title: 20m HAMR (Shuttle Run)
pt_help_details.section_content:
Run back and forth between two lines set 20 meters apart, in time with a series of beeps. You must touch the line with your foot on or before each beep.

The time between beeps gets shorter as the test progresses.

pt_help_details.title: 20m HAMR (Shuttle Run)
pt_help_details.section_content: If exempt, this component is excluded. The composite score is then calculated based only on the points earned in the remaining components, scaled to a 100-point maximum.

pt_help_details.title: 2-km Walk
pt_help_details.section_content: This component is not scored for points. You must complete the walk within the maximum time allowed for your age and gender to pass the cardio portion of the test.

pt_help_details.title: 2-km Walk
pt_help_details.section_content: If exempt, this component is excluded. The composite score is then calculated based only on the points earned in the remaining components, scaled to a 100-point maximum.

pt_help_details.title: Best Strength Score
pt_help_details.section_content: This section helps you find your highest possible score in the Strength category by comparing your personal bests across all available exercises. Enter your best performance for each exercise to see which one yields the most points.

pt_help_details.title: Best Core Score
pt_help_details.section_content: This section helps you find your highest possible score in the Core category by comparing your personal bests across all available exercises. Enter your best performance for each exercise to see which one yields the most points.

pt_help_details.title: Best Cardio Score
pt_help_details.section_content: This section helps you find your highest possible score in the Cardio category by comparing your personal bests across all available exercises. Enter your best performance for each exercise to see which one yields the most points.

pt_help_details.title: Altitude Adjustment
pt_help_details.section_content:
If you are testing at a location with an elevation of 5,000 feet or more above sea level, official adjustments are applied to cardio events to account for the reduced oxygen.

Select your altitude group to see these adjustments applied automatically.

pt_help_details.title: 1-Minute Push-ups
pt_help_details.section_content:
Start in the front-leaning rest position. Lower your body until your upper arms are at least parallel to the floor, then return to the starting position with your elbows fully extended.

Your body must remain in a straight line and not bow or arch.

pay_help_details.title: Hostile Fire Pay (HFP)
pay_help_details.purpose_description: For being subjected to hostile fire or a mine explosion (event-based).

pay_help_details.title: Imminent Danger Pay (IDP)
pay_help_details.purpose_description: For service in a designated imminent danger area (location-based).

pay_help_details.title: Hazardous Duty Incentive Pay (HDIP)
pay_help_details.purpose_description: For performing inherently hazardous duties (e.g., flying, parachuting, demolition).

pay_help_details.title: Hardship Duty Pay - Location (HDP-L)
pay_help_details.purpose_description: For assignment to locations with a poor quality of life.

pay_help_details.title: Assignment Incentive Pay (AIP)
pay_help_details.purpose_description: Flexible incentive for hard-to-fill assignments or to extend tours.

pay_help_details.title: Special Duty Assignment Pay (SDAP)
pay_help_details.purpose_description: Incentive for enlisted members in demanding jobs with high responsibility.

pay_help_details.title: Career Sea Pay
pay_help_details.purpose_description: For cumulative time spent on sea duty.

pay_help_details.title: Aviation Incentive Pays (AvIP)
pay_help_details.purpose_description: Incentive for rated aviators and aircrew to maintain proficiency.

pay_help_details.title: Health Professions Special Pays
pay_help_details.purpose_description: Bonuses and special pays to retain medical, dental, and nurse corps officers.

pay_help_details.title: Foreign Language Proficiency Bonus (FLPB)
pay_help_details.purpose_description: For maintaining proficiency in a foreign language critical to the military.

pay_help_details.title: Clothing Allowance
pay_help_details.purpose_description: Annual allowance for replacement of military uniforms.

pay_help_details.title: Servicemembers' Group Life Insurance (SGLI)
pay_help_details.purpose_description: Low-cost group life insurance for servicemembers.

pay_help_details.title: Thrift Savings Plan (TSP)
pay_help_details.purpose_description: A retirement savings and investment plan for Federal employees and members of the uniformed services.

pay_help_details.title: Additional Deductions
pay_help_details.purpose_description: Any other deductions from your pay.

pay_help_details.title: Pay Display Summary
pay_help_details.purpose_description: This display provides an estimated breakdown of your pay. It separates gross income from deductions to show how your net pay is calculated. It is designed to give you a clear picture of your total compensation, including both taxable and non-taxable income sources.
pay_help_details.calculation_details:
The calculation starts with your **base pay**, determined by your rank and years of service. It then adds any **special pays** and **allowances** you receive, such as Basic Allowance for Housing (BAH) and Basic Allowance for Subsistence (BAS).

If you have a VA disability rating, your tax-free **disability compensation** is also included. Finally, it subtracts estimated **deductions**, including federal and state taxes, FICA, and SGLI/TSP contributions, to arrive at your net pay.
pay_help_details.example:
**ANNUAL** and **MONTHLY** at the top show your *gross* (pre-tax) pay.

The **Income** column lists all sources of pay. The `paySource` (e.g., *Military* or *VA*) determines if it is taxable. Military pay is based on your rank and service, while VA Disability pay is tax-free.

The **Deductions** column shows estimated subtractions from your gross pay. The final pay amounts at the top reflect your total income *before* these deductions are taken out.
pay_help_details.report_section: The **Deductions** column shows estimated subtractions from your gross pay. The final pay amounts at the top reflect your total income *before* these deductions are taken out.

retirement_help_details.title: High-3
retirement_help_details.purpose_description: To calculate the average of your highest 36 months of basic pay, which is the foundation of your retirement pension.
retirement_help_details.calculation_details: The calculator takes the pay grades you select for your final three years of service, finds the corresponding basic pay for those years, and averages them to estimate your High-3 amount.
retirement_help_details.example: If your pay for the last three years was $60k, $62k, and $64k, your High-3 average would be $62k.

retirement_help_details.title: TSP
retirement_help_details.purpose_description: To estimate the future value of your Thrift Savings Plan investments.
retirement_help_details.calculation_details: The calculator uses the future value of a series formula based on your average salary, contribution percentage, years of contributing, and estimated annual rate of return. For BRS members, it also includes a 1% automatic government contribution and up to 4% matching.
retirement_help_details.example: If you contribute $100/month for 20 years with a 7% return, your estimated TSP balance would be over $52,000.

retirement_help_details.title: Qualifying Deployment Days
retirement_help_details.purpose_description: To determine if you are eligible for early retirement from the Guard or Reserves.
retirement_help_details.calculation_details: For every **90 days** of qualifying active duty service performed in a single fiscal year after January 28, 2008, your retirement age is reduced by **3 months**. The maximum reduction is to age **50**.
retirement_help_details.example: If you have 180 days of qualifying service, you can retire 6 months early (e.g., at age 59 and 6 months).

retirement_help_details.title: Service Points
retirement_help_details.purpose_description: To determine your retirement pay multiplier as a member of the Guard or Reserves.
retirement_help_details.calculation_details: You earn points for drills (**1 point** per drill), annual training (**1 point** per day), and other active duty. A minimum of **50 points** is required for a *good year*. Your total points are divided by **360** to determine your years of service for pay calculation.
retirement_help_details.example: If you have 4,000 points, your equivalent years of service would be 4000 / 360 = 11.11 years.

retirement_help_details.title: Good Years
retirement_help_details.purpose_description: To determine your eligibility for retirement as a member of the Guard or Reserves.
retirement_help_details.calculation_details: A *good year* is any year in which you earn at least **50 service points**. You must have **20 good years** to be eligible to receive retirement pay.
retirement_help_details.example: Even if you have 25 years in service, you will not be eligible for retirement pay if you have fewer than 20 'good years'.

retirement_help_details.title: Retirement Display Summary
retirement_help_details.purpose_description: This display provides an estimate of your future retirement pay based on the options you've selected. It shows the gross (pre-tax) amounts you can expect to receive annually and monthly.
retirement_help_details.calculation_details:
The calculation is based on several key factors: your years of service, your final pay grade, and the specific retirement plan you are under (e.g., High-36, BRS). This calculation applies to a standard active duty retirement, which **includes Active Guard Reserve (AGR) members**.

**Important Note on TSP:** This display shows your estimated monthly **pension only**. It does not include any income from your Thrift Savings Plan (TSP) account. Your total retirement income will be this pension *plus* any distributions you take from your TSP.
retirement_help_details.example: For instance, if you retire with 20 years of service under the High-36 plan, your pension is typically calculated as 50% (2.5% x 20 years) of the average of your highest 36 months of basic pay. This display applies that percentage to the estimated pay for your selected grade to arrive at the final figure.

retirement_help_details.title: Retirement Age
retirement_help_details.purpose_description: Determine your retirement pay eligibility date based on service component and history.
retirement_help_details.calculation_details:
• **Active Duty**: Begins immediately upon retirement (typ. 20+ YOS).

• **Guard / Reserves**: Begins at **age 60**.

• **Early Eligibility**: Reduced by **3 months for every 90 days** of qualifying active duty (post-2008), down to age **50**.
retirement_help_details.example: Active Duty members typically begin pay at retirement. Reservists begin at age 60, unless qualifying for early retirement (min. age 50).

best_score_help_details.title: Best Score Cardio
best_score_help_details.section_content: Enter your best times/reps for cardio exercises. The calculator will use your best performance from the 1.5-Mile Run, 20m HAMR, or 2-km Walk to achieve your highest possible PT score. Altitude adjustments will be applied if applicable.

best_score_help_details.title: Best Score Demographics
best_score_help_details.section_content: Your age and gender are used to determine the scoring standards for each PT component. Accurate demographic information ensures your best score is calculated against the correct Air Force fitness standards.

best_score_help_details.title: Best Score Strength
best_score_help_details.section_content: This section allows you to input your personal best for strength exercises. The system will automatically select the best performance between 1-Minute Push-ups and 2-Minute Hand-Release Push-ups to contribute to your overall best PT score.

best_score_help_details.title: Best Score Core
best_score_help_details.section_content: Input your best performance for core exercises. The system will determine the highest scoring exercise among 1-Minute Sit-ups, 2-Minute Cross-Leg Reverse Crunches, and Forearm Planks to maximize your overall best PT score.
